message:
fix(node): fail-closed fleet operation ID exhaustion and borrow-safe release

Redesign operation ID allocation to use a simple checked_add approach:
the terminal u64::MAX ID is issued once, then all subsequent allocations
fail closed with FLEET_OPERATION_ID_EXHAUSTED. This replaces the previous
epoch-rollover scheme that tried to keep IDs unique indefinitely, which
was unnecessarily complex and masked exhaustion rather than surfacing it.

Fix a borrow-checker hazard in the release path by splitting the
immutable read (to extract zone_id and action_type) from the mutable
mutation (to set status=Released). The operation ID is now allocated
before mutating incident state, so if allocation fails, no state has
been modified — true fail-closed semantics.

Test changes:
- Verify release fails closed before mutating state when IDs exhausted
- Verify terminal operation ID is issued once then allocator fails
- Update rollover/quarantine tests to match simplified fail-closed model

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
